item.title: About
item.description: Who am I My name is Moorsalin Munshi. I like to read about geopolitics and stocks, and knit as hobbies. I am a software engineer. What do I do I am currently employed as a software engineer at Eigen Technologies on their solutions engineering team. I graduated from Georgia Tech with a Masters of Science in Computer Science. This is the program I participated in here Courses I have completed at Georgia Tech CS 6262 Network Security CS 6035 Introduction to Information Security CS 6300 Software Development Process CS 6250 Computer Networks CS 7646 Machine Learning for Trading CS 6340 Software Analysis CS 6601 Artificial Intelligence CS 6400 Database Systems Concepts and Design CS 6238 Secure Computer Systems CS 6515 Intro to Graduate Algorithms

item.title: Grad school in review
item.description: Finally, after four long years I have completed my Masters program in computer science. What have I learned? Let us have a look: Courses I completed CS 6262 Network Security CS 6035 Introduction to Information Security CS 6300 Software Development Process CS 6250 Computer Networks CS 7646 Machine Learning for Trading CS 6340 Software Analysis CS 6601 Artificial Intelligence CS 6400 Database Systems Concepts and Design CS 6238 Secure Computer Systems CS 6515 Intro to Graduate Algorithms I suppose I should give summaries of the above courses.

item.title: Consuming Twitter API With Tweepy
item.description: Background Twitter is a veritable source of social media data. It is unhindered by the same privacy concerns as Facebook because of its cavalier attitude towards social networking and less intrusive data collection. Twitter has become quite popular amongst journalists since its inception, as well as politicians and other figures with political power. I am interested in the interactions journalists have on Twitter. There are two classes of users, blue checkmarks and non checkmarked.

item.title: Getting Started with Algorithmic Trading
item.description: Background In Summer 2019 I took a course on machine learning for trading. In the course, I learned that the general approach to algorithmic trading involves selecting some technical indicators and feeding them into machine learning models. A triggering signal is defined for buying or selling a stock. There are a few brokerages that provide APIs to their customers. I am using Alpaca, which provides API wrappers in Python, as well as Go, C#, and Javascript.

item.title: DIY Surveillance
item.description: Background I have a home surveillance kit I bought from Costco. It’s made by FLIR, who have a major market share in surveillance and optics in the US. The surveillance kit features software for viewing cameras, but its not very good.
